threads.topic: Using generic pressure sensors as MAP sensors
threads.summary: @Bearded_Bogan asked if generic pressure sensors could be used as Manifold Absolute Pressure (MAP) sensors. @epicEFIdev responded that while it's technically possible to use a generic pressure sensor for MAP sensing, the sensor must have the appropriate range and accuracy for engine management systems.
key_details: The question pertains to using generic pressure sensors in place of dedicated MAP sensors
@epicEFIdev emphasized the need for proper range and accuracy in the chosen sensor

TOPIC: Using generic pressure sensors as MAP sensors

- The question pertains to using generic pressure sensors in place of dedicated MAP sensors
- @epicEFIdev emphasized the need for proper range and accuracy in the chosen sensor

threads.topic: Adjusting VEAL's Max Cell Percentage Change for smoother operation
threads.summary: @MykkH discussed adjusting the Max Cell Percentage Change in VEAL to a small value (2-4%) to prevent overshooting the target VE values. This adjustment allows for incremental changes every 12 seconds instead of making large adjustments based on outdated data.
key_details: Max Cell Percentage Change set between 2% and 4%
VEAL updates every 12-15 seconds
Smaller incremental changes help avoid overshooting the target VE values
threads.raw_summary:
TOPIC: Adjusting VEAL's Max Cell Percentage Change for smoother operation
CONTRIBUTORS: @MykkH, @Joesphan
SUMMARY: @MykkH discussed adjusting the Max Cell Percentage Change in VEAL to a small value (2-4%) to prevent overshooting the target VE values. This adjustment allows for incremental changes every 12 seconds instead of making large adjustments based on outdated data.
KEY_DETAILS:
- Max Cell Percentage Change set between 2% and 4%
- VEAL updates every 12-15 seconds
- Smaller incremental changes help avoid overshooting the target VE values

threads.summary: User @MykkH discusses the adjustment of fuel tuning parameters, specifically mentioning that switching from "easy" to "normal" mode in the VE (Volumetric Efficiency) table improves performance. They note achieving a better approximation of target AFRs with a 2% max cell change in normal mode.

threads.topic: Locking Idle Cells for Stable Idle Operation
threads.summary: User @MykkH is experiencing issues when transitioning from overrun to idle, where the Air-Fuel Ratio (AFR) becomes unstable and shows large fluctuations due to oversized long tube headers. To address this, they plan to lock their idle cells for more stable operation.
key_details: Transitioning from overrun to idle causes instability in AFR
Large fluctuations are attributed to oversized long tube headers
Plan to lock idle cells to achieve a more stable idle operation
threads.raw_summary:
TOPIC: Locking Idle Cells for Stable Idle Operation
CONTRIBUTORS: @MykkH
SUMMARY: User @MykkH is experiencing issues when transitioning from overrun to idle, where the Air-Fuel Ratio (AFR) becomes unstable and shows large fluctuations due to oversized long tube headers. To address this, they plan to lock their idle cells for more stable operation.
KEY_DETAILS:
- Transitioning from overrun to idle causes instability in AFR
- Large fluctuations are attributed to oversized long tube headers
- Plan to lock idle cells to achieve a more stable idle operation

threads.topic: Adjustments for reducing pops and bangs during engine deceleration
threads.summary: The thread discusses methods to reduce popping and banging noises that occur during engine deceleration. @paleppp suggests leaning the VE ( volumetric efficiency ) table in overrun areas and setting the ignition advance for those same areas to 0. @MykkH confirms this approach by sharing a video demonstrating the issue.
key_details: Adjust the VE table to lean in overrun areas
Set the ignition advance to 0 in the same overrun areas
threads.raw_summary:
TOPIC: Adjustments for reducing pops and bangs during engine deceleration
CONTRIBUTORS: @paleppp, @MykkH
SUMMARY: The thread discusses methods to reduce popping and banging noises that occur during engine deceleration. @paleppp suggests leaning the VE ( volumetric efficiency ) table in overrun areas and setting the ignition advance for those same areas to 0. @MykkH confirms this approach by sharing a video demonstrating the issue.
KEY_DETAILS:
- Adjust the VE table to lean in overrun areas
- Set the ignition advance to 0 in the same overrun areas

threads.topic: Wideband Sensor Issues
threads.summary: User @Bloopdog reported that their wideband sensor is reading full lean after a replacement. Other users shared similar issues with sensors failing within weeks or months. @Bearded_Bogan suggested not turning the sensor on until the engine warms up to improve longevity. @Robb235 recommended checking the wiring connections in the AEM gauge pod and jiggling wires if using an AEM sensor.
key_details: Sensor reading full lean after replacement
Suggested to turn wideband sensor on only when engine is warm
Check wiring connections in AEM gauge pod for AEM sensors
threads.raw_summary:
TOPIC: Wideband Sensor Issues
CONTRIBUTORS: @Bloopdog, @MartyMotorsports, @Bearded_Bogan, @Robb235
SUMMARY: User @Bloopdog reported that their wideband sensor is reading full lean after a replacement. Other users shared similar issues with sensors failing within weeks or months. @Bearded_Bogan suggested not turning the sensor on until the engine warms up to improve longevity. @Robb235 recommended checking the wiring connections in the AEM gauge pod and jiggling wires if using an AEM sensor.
KEY_DETAILS:
- Sensor reading full lean after replacement
- Suggested to turn wideband sensor on only when engine is warm
- Check wiring connections in AEM gauge pod for AEM sensors

threads.topic: Delaying Engine Start Until Oil Pressure is Detected
threads.summary: The discussion revolves around the ability to delay engine startup until a sufficient oil pressure level is detected. This ensures that the engine does not start before adequate lubrication, potentially preventing wear and tear.
key_details: Delaying engine start until oil pressure is detected can be implemented as an option in the rusEFI/epicEFI system settings.
@Bearded_Bogan mentioned this feature as a way to protect the engine from starting without proper lubrication.

KEY_DETAILS:

- @Bearded_Bogan mentioned this feature as a way to protect the engine from starting without proper lubrication.

KEY_DETAILS:
- The logging of trigger inputs must be explicitly enabled
- This setting is found in the "Data Logging" section of the settings menu

threads.topic: Issue with spare MAP sensor reading magnetic interference instead of pressure
threads.summary: @Bearded_Bogan reported that a spare MAP (Manifold Absolute Pressure) sensor is reading magnetic interference when metal passes over it, rather than measuring the correct pressure. This indicates an issue with the sensor's functionality or wiring.
key_details: The spare MAP sensor reads magnetic interference instead of pressure readings
Metal passing over the sensor causes a change in its output

threads.topic: Troubleshooting a faulty input pin due to foreign object interference
threads.summary: @Bearded_Bogan reported an issue where a specific input pin on their rusEFI/epicEFI controller stopped functioning correctly. The problem was traced back to plastic debris getting stuck in the pin, causing intermittent contact.
key_details: Plastic debris interfered with proper pin function
Pin malfunction caused by physical obstruction
